description: Having or showing experience, knowledge, and good judgment. 'Phue' names the quality of deep understanding—not mere cleverness but genuine insight, the knowing that comes from experience and reflection.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes insight. 'Phu' /ɸu/ begins with thoughtful breath, wisdom gathering. Then 'e' opens to clarity, understanding revealed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phue serani' (wise elder). Central to Phi's philosophical vocabulary. Related to 'prajna' in Buddhist thought—wisdom that sees things as they are.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Wisdom (prajna) is one of the three trainings—seeing clearly the nature of reality, impermanence, and interconnection.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Elders were honored as wisdom-keepers—those whose experience had distilled into genuine understanding.
